plugins.name: typescript-lsp
plugins.description: TypeScript/JavaScript language server for Claude Code

plugins.name: second-opinion
plugins.description: Get 1-3 independent second opinions from other models to strengthen agent reasoning

plugins.name: preflight
plugins.description: Honest self-assessment that surfaces incomplete work, missing verifications, and improvement ideas before shipping

plugins.name: verify-code
plugins.description: Black-box end-to-end verification — actually runs the system and shows evidence that it works

plugins.name: verify-claims
plugins.description: Identify and verify unverified claims in Claude's own responses before presenting them as conclusions

plugins.name: pre-mortem
plugins.description: Systematically identify how a plan or design could fail before committing to it

plugins.name: design-options
plugins.description: Generate multiple strong design options with explicit tradeoff profiles, starting from the ideal

plugins.name: steel-man-own-position
plugins.description: Before flipping a previously-stated lean under pushback, restate the strongest version of your prior position and check whether the triggering argument actually refutes it

plugins.name: align
plugins.description: Surface what the agent thinks the task is before producing any artifact — a manifest of task interpretation, approach, source coverage, rendering choices, and blocking questions

plugins.name: silent-failures
plugins.description: Audit error handling for silent failures, inadequate feedback, and inappropriate fallbacks — delegates to a subagent for isolation

plugins.name: explore
plugins.description: Map the terrain before designing — locate, trace, pattern, constrain. Used before any substantive design or planning step. Output is mostly for the agent's grounding; only surprises and hard constraints surface

plugins.name: clarify
plugins.description: Surface and resolve underspecified decisions before designing — scope, edge behavior, trade-offs, integration, constraints, audience. Asks targeted questions with defaults so design choices aren't locked around hidden assumptions

plugins.name: handoff
plugins.description: Write a durable handoff for work that's about to ship — PR descriptions, summaries, memos, or memory notes. Tells the next reader what was built, why, what changed, and what's next, without forcing them to spelunk the diff
